MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-06-2024 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302

DISCOVERY 302, MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET NO ONE DOCUMENTS AND A SUPPLEMENTAL PRIVILEGE LOG TO DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO IS DENIED IN PART AND GRANTED IN PART. RFPS 2, 42, 43: DENIED. RFPS 14, 15, 21, 22: GRANTED. RFPS 24, 25, 26, 27: DENIED. RFPS 28-36: GRANTED. REQUEST FOR A SUPPLEMENTAL PRIVILEGE LOG IS DENIED. [SEE MINI MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLAINCE WITH CRC 3.1312 AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO JUDGE PRO TEM TOM COHEN AT TOM@THOMASCOHEN.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: TOM COHEN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/JPT)

Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set No One Documents And A Supplemental Privilege Log To Defendant City And County Of San Francisco
Off Calendar Mar-06-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MEI LIN'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set No One Documents And A Supplemental Privilege Log To Defendant City And County Of San Francisco

The Motion is denied in part and granted in part as follows:

RFPs 2, 42, 43: Denied. The reports are privileged as attorney-client communication and as attorney work product.

RFPs 14, 15, 21, 22: Granted for the reasons stated in Plaintiff's briefs.

RFPs 24, 25, 26, 27: Denied for the reasons stated in Defendants' brief.

RFPs 28-36: Granted as follows: The Requests as written are overbroad as to time. Unless the parties agree to a different time frame, each Request shall be deemed to be for documents going back 10 years from the date of the incident. Further, any Request seeking documents relating to "elderly" passengers shall be deemed to mean those over 60 years old-since that is the way Muni characterizes the "elderly." However, since each request asks for the "numbers" of incidents, unless Muni has compilation documents showing "numbers," there may not be any (or very few) responsive documents.

The request for a supplemental privilege log is denied.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in complaince with CRC 3.1312 and must e-mail it to Judge Pro Tem Tom Cohen at tom@thomascohen.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)
